entity.summary: Defunct cryptocurrency exchange founded in 2011 and taken offline in July 2017 after U.S. law-enforcement actions and the arrest of Alexander Vinnik. HEI records BTC-e as a dead event-backed exchange due to the 2017 indictment, FinCEN penalty, domain/service disruption, and later guilty plea by Vinnik.
entity.notes: Added as a fresh-discovery event-backed record rather than from the verified-unadded 1000-row backlog. The 2017 DOJ source is an indictment-era source and is recorded as allegations unless later resolved. The later Vinnik guilty plea is recorded separately and does not convert every 2017 allegation into an adjudicated fact. WEX is not merged into this record.

events.title: U.S. authorities charged BTC-e and Alexander Vinnik
events.description: The U.S. Department of Justice announced a 21-count indictment against BTC-e and Alexander Vinnik in July 2017, describing BTC-e as a large digital-currency exchange and alleging unlicensed money-service and money-laundering violations.
events.notes: The event records the indictment and related law-enforcement action. Allegations are not treated as adjudicated facts unless supported by later resolution sources.

events.title: BTC-e service went offline during law-enforcement action
events.description: BTC-e went offline around the July 2017 law-enforcement action. Public reference material and reporting describe the 2017 outage/seizure period as the end of the original BTC-e service.
events.notes: The original BTC-e service is treated as dead. WEX-related successor activity is not merged into the canonical BTC-e entity.

events.title: Alexander Vinnik pleaded guilty to money-laundering conspiracy
events.description: The U.S. Department of Justice announced in May 2024 that Alexander Vinnik pleaded guilty to conspiracy to commit money laundering in connection with BTC-e's operation.
events.notes: Resolution event for Vinnik. This does not imply the exchange itself resumed or that all earlier allegations were adjudicated.

events.title: BTC-e added as fresh-discovery historical exchange
events.description: BTC-e was added as a fresh-discovery historical exchange record because of strong event-backed public evidence and historical significance.
events.notes: No verified-unadded backlog row was consumed.

evidence.title: Russian National And Bitcoin Exchange Charged In 21-Count Indictment

evidence.notes: DOJ source for the 2017 indictment, arrest context, BTC-e description, and FinCEN penalty reference.

evidence.notes: Republication/contextual source for the DOJ statement.

evidence.notes: Used only as a temporary public reference for service offline / closure chronology. Replace later with archived BTC-e notices or primary domain-seizure documentation if available.

evidence.notes: Supports the law-enforcement action context that made the original BTC-e service non-operational.

evidence.notes: DOJ resolution source for Vinnik's guilty plea in connection with BTC-e.
